prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe, 10 C, 10 H) performed with the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme at the GFN2-xTB level for the neutral (N), cation (N−1 electron), and anion (N+1 electron) states, and Fukui indices derived by finite difference. Geometry used as-is from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (no re-optimization). No solvent model applied (gas phase). Software: xtb (GFN2-xTB).

The headline result (f⁺ for the most electrophilic site) is 0.22885456, located on the iron centre (atom 11, 1-based). The iron centre is also the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.25938191. The dual descriptor on Fe is −0.030527354, confirming Fe as the dominant nucleophilic site. All Cp ring carbons and hydrogens carry small, symmetry-equivalent Fukui values (~0.01364 for C, ~0.0635 for H). The f⁺ and f⁻ sums both equal 1.0000, confirming charge conservation. Integrity status: trustworthy = true. No warnings reported by the engine.
